schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: re coordinates as $(\Theta, \Phi; \Upsilon)$ where $(\Theta,\Phi)$ are the usual polar and azimuthal angles, and $\Upsilon \in {+1,-1}$ indicates the branch (TZP⁺ or TZP⁻). Here $+1$ might correspond to flux emerging (TZP⁺) and $-1$ to flux entering (TZP⁻). So, for example, the north geographic pole direction might be labeled $(\Theta=0, \Upsilon=-1)$ if we consider field lines entering there (i.e., north magnetic pole is flux entering Earth), and the south geographic pole would be $(\Theta=\pi, \Upsilon=+1)$ if flux emerges. All along the equator ( $\Theta = \pi/2$ , $0 \le \Phi < 2\pi$ ), field lines from TZP⁺ and TZP⁻ mix, but we can label each direction twice: once as $(\pi/2,\Phi; +1)$ meaning the direction on the + sheet (coming from TZP⁺ originally), and $(\pi/2,\Phi; -1)$ meaning the same spatial direction but corresponding to field that came from TZP⁻. In free space far from TZP, field
